flavorPhrases: Tangled in the root system of a half-drowned cypress, just above the waterline -- {container}.
A hollow log, bobbing at the edge of the reed bed. Clay-sealed at both ends. Still dry.
Buried in the silt at the bank's edge, the oilskin wrapping intact despite everything.
Lashed to a submerged stake with rotting cord. The water kept it cool. The seal held.
Wedged in the fork of a long-dead tree, rotted hollow above the mud line.
The mud here has a different texture -- packed, not settled. Whatever is beneath it was placed deliberately.
